title: quercus erucifolia subsp. longipes
intro: Quercus erucifolia subsp. longipes, also known as the Mexican oak or the long-stalked Mexican oak, is a subspecies of evergreen oak tree native to Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Quercus erucifolia subsp. longipes include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and prevent certain diseases such as diabetes and cancer. Herbal preparations of the plant, including tea and extracts, are used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
